name: Single-dose tadalafil lowered blood pressure and arterial stiffness markers in grade 1 obesity but did not change FMD
statement: In participants with grade 1 obesity, a single tadalafil 20 mg dose acutely lowered peripheral and central blood pressure, augmentation index, and brachial-ankle pulse wave velocity, but did not modify flow-mediated dilation.
quantitative_note: SBP changed from 130.6+/-17.1 to 125.0+/-12.7 mm Hg (P=0.011), DBP from 82.7+/-18.2 to 76.5+/-11.8 mm Hg (P<=0.001), central SBP from 116.33+/-19.16 to 109.90+/-15.05 mm Hg (P=0.001), augmentation index from 69.1+/-17.1 to 65.7+/-14.4 (P=0.012), and brachial-ankle pulse wave velocity from 1229.7+/-218.4 to 1164.0+/-181.7 (P=0.001).

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports blood pressure, augmentation index, pulse wave velocity, and FMD results.

caveats: Obesity population rather than mostly healthy users.
Blood-pressure lowering can be beneficial or risky depending on baseline BP, interacting drugs, alcohol, heat stress, and volume status.
population_profile: Adults with grade 1 obesity; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
endpoint_measure: Peripheral and central blood pressure, augmentation index, brachial-ankle pulse wave velocity, and flow-mediated dilation.
effect_estimate: Acute reductions in SBP, DBP, central SBP, augmentation index, and baPWV; no FMD modification.
safety_findings: Abstract does not report adverse events; hemodynamic lowering should be framed with nitrate, alpha-blocker, antihypertensive, alcohol, and cardiovascular-status cautions.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for acute hemodynamic response; low for long-term healthy-user cardiovascular benefit.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Acute single dose, surrogate endpoints, no clinical event outcomes, and obesity-specific physiology.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is acute dose timing and obesity context.
